seo.title: Vercel AI SDK Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Vercel AI SDK agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using Vercel AI SDK face heightened risks when building AI-powered patient interfaces, as streaming responses and tool calls can inadvertently expose PHI or generate unauditable medical advice. Non-compliance with HIPAA, HITECH, and FDA regulations can lead to patient harm, fines up to $1.5M per violation, and loss of medical licenses. Vex provides essential guardrails by validating outputs, ensuring data residency, and creating immutable audit trails for every AI interaction.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires PHI to remain within approved environments and mandates de-identification before AI processing
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces data residency policies and PHI redaction before LLM input, with audit logs proving compliance

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records be trustworthy, auditable, and protected from unauthorized changes
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Creates immutable audit trails for all AI decisions with tamper-proof signatures and validation proofs

complianceRequirements.description: Imposes breach notification requirements and strengthens HIPAA enforcement for PHI handling
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Real-time PHI detection blocks breaches and generates compliance-ready incident reports

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls for security, availability, and confidentiality over 6+ months
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Provides continuous monitoring, automated evidence collection, and SOC 2-compliant audit APIs

industryRisks.name: Streaming Hallucinations with PHI
industryRisks.description: Partial streaming responses generate incorrect medical advice containing patient data before validation
industryRisks.example: AI suggests wrong dosage mid-stream while mentioning patient name, violating HIPAA

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Tool Call Results
industryRisks.description: External API calls return unverified medical data displayed to clinicians without validation
industryRisks.example: Lab results tool returns fabricated values shown in patient dashboard

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Structured Outputs
industryRisks.description: Different LLM providers return varying JSON schemas for diagnosis codes, breaking audit chains
industryRisks.example: OpenAI formats ICD-10 differently than Anthropic, causing record validation failures

industryRisks.name: Edge Timeout on Complex Tasks
industryRisks.description: Long-running agent tasks timeout on Vercel Edge, losing audit context mid-diagnosis
industryRisks.example: Multi-step differential diagnosis times out, orphaning partial PHI processing

implementation.title: StreamText with Real-time Hallucination Guardrails
implementation.description: Protects streaming UIs from partial hallucinations with token-level validation

implementation.title: Structured Tool Calls with Audit Validation
implementation.description: Ensures tool call results are validated and auditable before clinical display

caseStudyScenario.heading: Vercel AI SDK in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Sarah's telehealth platform at Metro Health uses Vercel AI SDK to power a streaming symptom checker for 50K monthly patients. The React Server Component UI streams real-time triage advice using Claude 3.5 Sonnet, pulling lab results via tool calls to their Epic EMR integration. During peak flu season, complex differential diagnosis workflows began timing out on Vercel Edge functions, orphaning PHI processing and generating partial hallucinated advice like 'Consider thrombolysis for chest pain' before validation.

Without guardrails, a patient's PHI was streamed unredacted, and inconsistent structured outputs across providers broke their audit trail for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. Regulators flagged the system after a near-miss where unvalidated tool results displayed fabricated troponin levels, risking inappropriate cardiac catheterization.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex middleware integration took 2 hours. PHI redaction blocked 98% of leaks at input, while real-time hallucination detection flagged 47 dangerous recommendations during streams. Immutable audit trails proved every clinical decision was validated against ICD-10 schemas with >90% confidence. Post-deployment, Metro Health passed their HIPAA audit with zero findings and reduced malpractice exposure by 83%. The system now handles 200% more concurrent streams without timeouts, maintaining full auditability.

cta.heading: Comply with Healthcare Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Vercel AI SDK in 5 minutes to eliminate PHI leaks and ensure auditable medical AI.
